title: Swim in Rising Water
description:
You are given an n x n integer matrix grid where each value grid[i][j] represents the elevation at that point (i, j).

The rain starts to fall. At time t, the depth of the water everywhere is t. You can swim from a square to another 4-directionally adjacent square if and only if both squares have an elevation less than or equal to t. You can swim infinite distances in zero time. Of course, you must stay within the boundaries of the grid during your swim.

You start at the top left square (0, 0). What is the least time until you can reach the bottom right square (n - 1, n - 1)?
input_format: The first line contains n. The next n lines each contain n integers separated by space.
